ideation: This sequence will depict a baby chick pecking at the ground for food, then looking up inquisitively.  The movement will be primarily focused on the head and neck, but I will use subtle arm movements to enhance the overall effect.  The arms will remain largely still, mirroring the chick's small size and the fact that a chick doesn't use its wings to move in this way.  The overall effect should be cute and endearing. The first frame shows a neutral starting position. The second frame shows the head lowered as if pecking, with the arms slightly moving forward to suggest a gentle approach. The third frame shows the head lifting back up, with the arms returning to a neutral position. The fourth frame shows a slight pause, with the head tilted slightly upwards, suggesting curiosity.  The fifth frame returns the arms and head to the initial neutral position.

frames.beat: arms hang low, ready to peck
arms move slightly forward, head dips down to peck
head lifts up, arms return to neutral
head tilts up, looking around curiously
arms and head return to rest
